fault_hypothesis: On line 72, using `int(x)` (floor truncation) instead of proper rounding causes the sum of integer allocations to be less than `total` when fractional parts are truncated away. The fix requires a rounding strategy that preserves the total (e.g., largest-remainder method).

constraints.intent: After integer truncation, the sum of allocations must still equal total — this will be FALSE on failing tests where truncation loses units.

spec.expr: sum(allocations__1) == sum(int(x) for x in planned__1) and sum(allocations__1) == int(total)

constraints.intent: After defining allocations__1 via int() truncation, its sum must equal total — violated when fractional parts are discarded.
Before consuming planned__1, verify its sum equals total so any loss is attributable to the int() conversion on line 72.
At the end of the integer-allocation branch, the total must be preserved — failing when floor truncation drops units.
The returned allocations must sum to total — a symptom-level check that catches the rounding bug.
